mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, lower short-rate expectations reduce discount rates and support equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days. The signal is valid only when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because easing during reaccelerating inflation is likely to be interpreted as stress rather than support.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below baseline in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is declining and CPIAUCSL is flat-to-down, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals a weakening labor market and higher recession probability, which should pressure cyclical earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should strengthen when PAYEMS is also slowing, confirming labor deterioration rather than a noisy unemployment move.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of signals despite UNRATE rising and PAYEMS slowing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals contraction in housing demand and broader private-sector activity, which should lead equities lower over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised down. The mechanism fails if housing weakens while retail sales proxies are stable, implying the housing move is sector-specific rather than macro-wide.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative following HOUST declines above the threshold, or if the signal loses directional accuracy relative to baseline over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates real output is improving without an inflation shock, which should support risk assets and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when industrial production is above its 3-month average, showing actual acceleration rather than one-off noise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20-day SPX returns do not dominate when INDPRO is above trend and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, or if the signal's hit rate falls below 50% in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 5 trading days. The signal is most reliable when CPIAUCSL remains elevated, because restrictive policy paired with firm inflation leaves the Fed less room to ease quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in most periods after FEDFUNDS crosses the threshold, or if the triggered-signal Sharpe ratio is not negative over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumption and revenue momentum, which should lift expected earnings and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when higher sales are offset by rising unemployment, because weak household labor income would make the sales improvement unsustainable.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS does not coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes negative whenever UNRATE is not improving.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is falling and UNRATE is rising together, the labor market is deteriorating in a way that directly increases recession odds and should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 60 trading days. This is a regime signal for recessionary labor contraction rather than a simple monthly noise effect.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up condition fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or if positive returns occur in at least 40% of such episodes over a rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO combined with improving HOUST signals broadening real activity across production and construction, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is falsified if the recovery in production is not accompanied by housing improvement, because then the growth impulse is too narrow to sustain equity gains.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 3-month average after a prior decline
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in most periods when INDPRO strengthens and HOUST recovers, or if the joint signal underperforms a zero benchmark over any 2-year rolling window.
